192	// beadReviewInstructions is the review contract embedded in the prompt.
193	// The reviewing agent must produce a single JSON object matching the
194	// ReviewVerdict schema (schema_version: 1). The markdown contract this
195	// replaces silently mis-parsed `### Verdict: APPROVE` outputs whenever the
196	// model echoed the prompt's options header — see review_verdict.go.
197	const beadReviewInstructions = `You are reviewing a bead implementation against its acceptance criteria.
198	
199	For each acceptance-criteria (AC) item, decide whether it is implemented correctly, then assign one overall verdict:
200	
201	- APPROVE — every AC item is fully and correctly implemented.
202	- REQUEST_CHANGES — some AC items are partial or have fixable minor issues.
203	- BLOCK — at least one AC item is not implemented or incorrectly implemented; or the diff is insufficient to evaluate.
204	
205	## Required output format (schema_version: 1)
206	
207	Respond with EXACTLY one JSON object as your final response, fenced as a single ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` code block. Do not include any prose outside the fenced block. The JSON must match this schema:
208	
209	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json
210	{
211	  "schema_version": 1,
212	  "verdict": "APPROVE",
213	  "summary": "≤300 char human-readable verdict justification",
214	  "findings": [
215	    { "severity": "info", "summary": "what is wrong or notable", "location": "path/to/file.go:42" }
216	  ]
217	}
218	` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `
219	
220	Rules:
221	- "verdict" must be exactly one of "APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES", "BLOCK".
222	- "severity" must be exactly one of "info", "warn", "block".
223	- Output the JSON object inside ONE fenced ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + `json … ` + "`" + `` + "`" + `` + "`" + ` block. No additional prose, no extra fences, no markdown headings.
224	- Do not echo this template back. Do not write the words APPROVE, REQUEST_CHANGES, or BLOCK anywhere except as the JSON value of the verdict field.`

226	// BuildReviewPromptOptions configures evidence-bounded prompt assembly.
227	// FEAT-022 §5/§7: caps drive per-section trimming and the pre-dispatch
228	// short-circuit on residual oversize.
229	type BuildReviewPromptOptions struct {
230		Caps evidence.Caps
231	}

238	// BuildReviewPromptResult is the structured output of BuildReviewPromptBounded.
239	// Overflow is true when, after all per-section trimming, the assembled
240	// prompt still exceeds Caps.MaxPromptBytes; callers MUST NOT dispatch in
241	// that case (FEAT-022 §7).
242	type BuildReviewPromptResult struct {
243		Prompt   string
244		Overflow bool
245		Sections []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection
246	}

248	// BuildReviewPrompt builds the complete review prompt for a bead implementation
249	// using default caps. Callers needing overflow detection use
250	// BuildReviewPromptBounded.
251	func BuildReviewPrompt(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
252		return BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
253	}
254	
255	// BuildReviewPromptWithProse builds the review prompt and includes an
256	// advisory prose-quality section derived from the bead text using the same
257	// docprose checker and config path as `ddx doc prose`.
258	func BuildReviewPromptWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef) string {
259		return BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: evidence.DefaultCaps()}).Prompt
260	}

262	// BuildReviewPromptBounded assembles the review prompt under byte caps using
263	// the cli/internal/evidence primitives. Governing documents are read via
264	// evidence.ReadFileClamped, the diff is decomposed and clamped via
265	// evidence.ClampDiff, and per-file inlining is bounded by
266	// Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes. The minimum evidence floor (bead title,
267	// description, acceptance, notes, and the full changed-file inventory) is
268	// preserved verbatim regardless of cap pressure (FEAT-022 §5).
269	func BuildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
270		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, false)
271	}
272	
273	// BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse assembles the review prompt with the
274	// optional prose-quality section enabled.
275	func BuildReviewPromptBoundedWithProse(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions) BuildReviewPromptResult {
276		return buildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, rev, diff, projectRoot, refs, opts, true)
277	}

279	func buildReviewPromptBounded(b *bead.Bead, iter int, rev, diff, projectRoot string, refs []GoverningRef, opts BuildReviewPromptOptions, includeProse bool) BuildReviewPromptResult {
280		caps := opts.Caps
281		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
282			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
283		}
284	
285		var sb strings.Builder
286		sections := []evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}
287	
288		sb.WriteString("<bead-review>\n")
289	
290		// ── Bead section (floor) ────────────────────────────────────────────────
291		beadStart := sb.Len()
292		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <bead id=%q iter=%d>\n", b.ID, iter)
293		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <title>%s</title>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.TrimSpace(b.Title)))
294	
295		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
296			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <description>\n%s\n    </description>\n", reviewXMLEscape(desc))
297		} else {
298			sb.WriteString("    <description/>\n")
299		}
300	
301		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
302			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <acceptance>\n%s\n    </acceptance>\n", reviewXMLEscape(acc))
303		} else {
304			sb.WriteString("    <acceptance/>\n")
305		}
306	
307		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
308			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <notes>\n%s\n    </notes>\n", reviewXMLEscape(notes))
309		}
310	
311		if len(b.Labels) > 0 {
312			fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <labels>%s</labels>\n", reviewXMLEscape(strings.Join(b.Labels, ", ")))
313		}
314	
315		sb.WriteString("  </bead>\n\n")
316		sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
317			Name:          "bead",
318			BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - beadStart,
319			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
320		})
321	
322		// ── Changed-file inventory (floor) ──────────────────────────────────────
323		allFiles := evidence.DecomposeDiff(diff)
324		if len(allFiles) > 0 {
325			invStart := sb.Len()
326			sb.WriteString("  <changed-files>\n")
327			paths := make([]string, 0, len(allFiles))
328			for _, f := range allFiles {
329				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <file>%s</file>\n", reviewXMLEscape(f.Path))
330				paths = append(paths, f.Path)
331			}
332			sb.WriteString("  </changed-files>\n\n")
333			sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
334				Name:          "changed-files",
335				BytesIncluded: sb.Len() - invStart,
336				SelectedItems: paths,
337			})
338		}
339	
340		// ── Governing docs section (clamped per-doc) ────────────────────────────
341		sb.WriteString("  <governing>\n")
342		if len(refs) == 0 {
343			sb.WriteString("    <note>No governing documents found. Evaluate the diff against the acceptance criteria alone.</note>\n")
344		} else {
345			for _, ref := range refs {
346				if ref.Title != "" {
347					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q title=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path, ref.Title)
348				} else {
349					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <ref id=%q path=%q>\n", ref.ID, ref.Path)
350				}
351				docPath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, filepath.FromSlash(ref.Path))
352				content, truncated, originalBytes, readErr := evidence.ReadFileClamped(docPath, caps.MaxGoverningDocBytes)
353				if readErr != nil {
354					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <note>Could not read %s: %s</note>\n", ref.Path, readErr)
355					sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
356						Name:             "governing:" + ref.ID,
357						TruncationReason: "read_error",
358						OmittedItems:     []string{ref.Path},
359					})
360				} else {
361					txt := strings.TrimSpace(string(content))
362					if truncated {
363						txt += evidence.TruncationMarker
364					}
365					fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <content>\n%s\n      </content>\n", evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(txt))
366					sec := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
367						Name:          "governing:" + ref.ID,
368						BytesIncluded: len(content),
369						SelectedItems: []string{ref.Path},
370					}
371					if truncated {
372						sec.TruncationReason = "governing_doc_cap"
373						sec.BytesOmitted = int(originalBytes) - len(content)
374					}
375					sections = append(sections, sec)
376				}
377				sb.WriteString("    </ref>\n")
378			}
379		}
380		sb.WriteString("  </governing>\n\n")
381	
382		// ── Diff section (ranked, per-file inlined cap, total clamp) ────────────
383		rankedDiff := rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(allFiles, diff, b, refs, caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes)
384		clampedDiff, diffSection := evidence.ClampDiff(rankedDiff, caps.MaxDiffBytes)
385		diffSection.Name = "diff"
386		sections = append(sections, diffSection)
387		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <diff rev=%q>\n%s\n  </diff>\n\n", rev, evidence.DelimitUntrustedData(strings.TrimRight(clampedDiff, "\n")))
388	
389		if includeProse {
390			proseSection, proseSectionEvidence, proseErr := buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot, b)
391			if proseErr != nil {
392				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <prose-review advisory=%q>\n", "true")
393				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <note>Could not compute prose findings: %s</note>\n", reviewXMLEscape(proseErr.Error()))
394				sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>\n\n")
395				sections = append(sections, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
396					Name:          "prose-review",
397					SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
398				})
399			} else {
400				sb.WriteString(proseSection)
401				sb.WriteString("\n")
402				sections = append(sections, proseSectionEvidence)
403			}
404		}
405	
406		// ── Instructions section ────────────────────────────────────────────────
407		instructions := strings.ReplaceAll(beadReviewInstructions, "<bead-id>", b.ID)
408		instructions = strings.ReplaceAll(instructions, "<N>", fmt.Sprintf("%d", iter))
409		if includeProse {
410			instructions += "\n\nIf a <prose-review> section is present, add a prose_findings array to the JSON output. Keep prose findings separate from correctness findings. Prose findings are advisory by default and must not change the overall verdict."
411		}
412		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "  <instructions>\n%s\n  </instructions>\n", reviewXMLEscape(instructions))
413	
414		sb.WriteString("</bead-review>\n")
415	
416		out := sb.String()
417		assembled := evidence.AssembleInline([]evidence.SectionInput{{
418			Name:    "review-prompt",
419			Content: out,
420		}}, len(out))
421		return BuildReviewPromptResult{
422			Prompt:   assembled.Prompt,
423			Overflow: len(out) > caps.MaxPromptBytes,
424			Sections: sections,
425		}
426	}

428	func buildProseReviewSection(projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead) (string, evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection, error) {
429		cfg, err := ddxconfig.LoadWithWorkingDir(projectRoot)
430		if err != nil {
431			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
432		}
433	
434		settings, err := docprose.ResolveSettings(cfg)
435		if err != nil {
436			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
437		}
438		checker, err := docprose.NewChecker(settings.Mode, settings.Vocabulary)
439		if err != nil {
440			return "", evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{}, err
441		}
442	
443		var src strings.Builder
444		if title := strings.TrimSpace(b.Title); title != "" {
445			fmt.Fprintf(&src, "# %s\n\n", title)
446		}
447		if desc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Description); desc != "" {
448			src.WriteString(desc)
449			src.WriteString("\n\n")
450		}
451		if acc := strings.TrimSpace(b.Acceptance); acc != "" {
452			src.WriteString("## Acceptance\n\n")
453			src.WriteString(acc)
454			src.WriteString("\n\n")
455		}
456		if notes := strings.TrimSpace(b.Notes); notes != "" {
457			src.WriteString("## Notes\n\n")
458			src.WriteString(notes)
459			src.WriteString("\n")
460		}
461	
462		findings := checker.Findings("bead.md", src.String())
463		var sb strings.Builder
464		sb.WriteString("  <prose-review advisory=\"true\">\n")
465		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <mode>%s</mode>\n", settings.Mode)
466		fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "    <policy>%s</policy>\n", settings.Policy)
467		if len(findings) == 0 {
468			sb.WriteString("    <findings/>\n")
469		} else {
470			sb.WriteString("    <findings>\n")
471			for _, finding := range findings {
472				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "      <finding file=%q line=%d rule_id=%q severity=%q>\n", finding.File, finding.Line, finding.RuleID, finding.Severity)
473				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <rationale>%s</rationale>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.Rationale))
474				fmt.Fprintf(&sb, "        <suggested_edit>%s</suggested_edit>\n", reviewXMLEscape(finding.SuggestedEdit))
475				sb.WriteString("      </finding>\n")
476			}
477			sb.WriteString("    </findings>\n")
478		}
479		sb.WriteString("  </prose-review>")
480	
481		section := evidence.EvidenceAssemblySection{
482			Name:          "prose-review",
483			SelectedItems: []string{"bead"},
484		}
485		section.BytesIncluded = len(src.String())
486		return sb.String(), section, nil
487	}

489	// rankAndDegradeDiffForReview reorders diff files so that AC-referenced files
490	// appear first, then files matching governing-ref paths, then others; and
491	// degrades any individual file body that exceeds maxInlinedFileBytes to
492	// "stat + hunk headers only" so a single oversize file cannot dominate the
493	// diff section. The returned string is a re-serialized unified diff suitable
494	// for evidence.ClampDiff. FEAT-022 §1.
495	func rankAndDegradeDiffForReview(files []evidence.DiffFile, originalDiff string, b *bead.Bead, refs []GoverningRef, maxInlinedFileBytes int) string {
496		if len(files) == 0 {
497			return originalDiff
498		}
499		rank := func(f evidence.DiffFile) int {
500			if f.Path == "" {
501				return 3
502			}
503			if mentionsPathLike(b.Acceptance, f.Path) {
504				return 0
505			}
506			for _, r := range refs {
507				if pathsRelated(r.Path, f.Path) {
508					return 1
509				}
510			}
511			return 2
512		}
513		ordered := make([]evidence.DiffFile, len(files))
514		copy(ordered, files)
515		sort.SliceStable(ordered, func(i, j int) bool { return rank(ordered[i]) < rank(ordered[j]) })
516	
517		var b2 strings.Builder
518		for _, f := range ordered {
519			body := f.Body
520			if maxInlinedFileBytes > 0 && len(body) > maxInlinedFileBytes {
521				body = degradeDiffFileBody(f)
522			}
523			b2.WriteString(body)
524		}
525		return b2.String()
526	}
527	
528	// degradeDiffFileBody returns a stat + hunk-headers only rendering of a diff
529	// file (FEAT-022 §1: large-file degradation). Used when a single file's body
530	// exceeds Caps.MaxInlinedFileBytes — preserves enough structure for the
531	// reviewer to know what changed without inlining the full content.
532	func degradeDiffFileBody(f evidence.DiffFile) string {
533		var b strings.Builder
534		b.WriteString("diff --git a/")
535		b.WriteString(f.Path)
536		b.WriteString(" b/")
537		b.WriteString(f.Path)
538		b.WriteString("\n")
539		if f.Stat != "" {
540			b.WriteString(f.Stat)
541			b.WriteString("\n")
542		}
543		for _, h := range f.HunkHeaders {
544			b.WriteString(h)
545			b.WriteString("\n")
546		}
547		b.WriteString("[…file body omitted by ddx evidence cap…]\n")
548		return b.String()
549	}

551	// mentionsPathLike returns true when text mentions the file path or its
552	// basename (a coarse heuristic — the reviewer only needs ranking, not
553	// exact symbolic resolution).
554	func mentionsPathLike(text, p string) bool {
555		if text == "" || p == "" {
556			return false
557		}
558		if strings.Contains(text, p) {
559			return true
560		}
561		base := path.Base(p)
562		return base != "" && base != "." && strings.Contains(text, base)
563	}
564	
565	// pathsRelated returns true when refPath and filePath share a directory
566	// prefix or basename, used to associate diff files with governing
567	// documents.
568	func pathsRelated(refPath, filePath string) bool {
569		if refPath == "" || filePath == "" {
570			return false
571		}
572		refDir := path.Dir(refPath)
573		if refDir != "" && refDir != "." && strings.HasPrefix(filePath, refDir+"/") {
574			return true
575		}
576		if path.Base(refPath) == path.Base(filePath) {
577			return true
578		}
579		return false
580	}

582	// reviewXMLEscape escapes &, <, and > for inclusion in XML text content.
583	func reviewXMLEscape(s string) string {
584		var buf bytes.Buffer
585		buf.WriteString(strings.NewReplacer(
586			"&", "&",
587			"<", "<",
588			">", ">",
589		).Replace(s))
590		return buf.String()
591	}

593	// DefaultBeadReviewer implements BeadReviewer by dispatching the review
594	// invocation through the agent service (or a test-injected runner).
595	// It fetches the bead, builds the review prompt, and runs the reviewer agent.
596	type DefaultBeadReviewer struct {
597		ProjectRoot string
598		BeadStore   BeadReader
599		// Service, when non-nil, is the agentlib.FizeauService used to dispatch the
600		// review invocation. Production callers leave this nil — ReviewBead
601		// constructs a fresh service from ProjectRoot via NewServiceFromWorkDir.
602		Service agentlib.FizeauService
603		// Runner, when non-nil, replaces the service-based dispatch path. Used by
604		// tests to return canned *Result values without spinning up a real
605		// service. Takes precedence over Service.
606		Runner AgentRunner
607		// Harness and Model override the reviewer harness/model.
608		// When Harness is empty, the reviewer follows the implementation harness;
609		// when both are empty, dispatch is left to the agent service.
610		Harness string
611		Model   string
612		// Caps configures the per-section evidence caps used when assembling
613		// the review prompt (FEAT-022). When zero-valued, evidence.DefaultCaps
614		// applies.
615		Caps evidence.Caps
616		// BeadEvents, when non-nil, is used to append the
617		// kind:review-pairing-degraded event when the reviewer's resolved
618		// provider matches the implementer's. Optional: nil disables the
619		// telemetry emission while preserving the rest of the review flow.
620		BeadEvents BeadEventAppender
621	}

623	// BuildReviewExecuteRequest constructs the AgentRunRuntime used for the
624	// post-merge reviewer dispatch. It pins the reviewer harness/model, attaches
625	// the implementer's correlation map (with role=reviewer overlaid), and
626	// derives MinPower as impl.ActualPower + 1 so routing is biased toward a
627	// stronger candidate than the implementer's actual selection. The returned
628	// runtime is missing per-call plumbing (Prompt/PromptFile, WorkDir,
629	// SessionLogDirOverride) — the caller fills those in before dispatching.
630	func BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerModel string) AgentRunRuntime {
631		return BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl, reviewerHarness, reviewerModel, ReviewGroupDispatchMeta{})
632	}
633	
634	// BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest constructs the reviewer runtime for one
635	// slot in a review group. It threads the implementer's correlation facts,
636	// overlays reviewer role metadata, and stamps the review_group_id and
637	// reviewer_index when provided so downstream routing / tracing can join the
638	// two reviewer slots back to the same evidence bundle.
639	func BuildReviewGroupExecuteRequest(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerModel string, meta ReviewGroupDispatchMeta) AgentRunRuntime {
640		correlation := map[string]string{}
641		for k, v := range impl.Correlation {
642			correlation[k] = v
643		}
644		correlation["role"] = "reviewer"
645		if meta.GroupID != "" {
646			correlation["review_group_id"] = meta.GroupID
647			correlation["reviewer_index"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", meta.ReviewerIndex)
648		}
649		if impl.Harness != "" {
650			correlation["impl_harness"] = impl.Harness
651		}
652		if impl.Provider != "" {
653			correlation["impl_provider"] = impl.Provider
654		}
655		if impl.Model != "" {
656			correlation["impl_model"] = impl.Model
657		}
658		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
659			correlation["impl_actual_power"] = fmt.Sprintf("%d", impl.ActualPower)
660		}
661		minPower := 0
662		if impl.ActualPower > 0 {
663			minPower = impl.ActualPower + 1
664		}
665		correlationID := reviewCorrelationID(correlation)
666		return AgentRunRuntime{
667			HarnessOverride:     reviewerHarness,
668			ModelOverride:       reviewerModel,
669			MinPowerOverride:    minPower,
670			Correlation:         correlation,
671			Role:                "reviewer",
672			CorrelationID:       correlationID,
673			PermissionsOverride: PermissionsReadOnlyReviewer,
674		}
675	}
676	
677	func reviewCorrelationID(correlation map[string]string) string {
678		if len(correlation) == 0 {
679			return ""
680		}
681		parts := make([]string, 0, 4)
682		if beadID := correlation["bead_id"]; beadID != "" {
683			parts = append(parts, beadID)
684		}
685		if groupID := correlation["review_group_id"]; groupID != "" {
686			parts = append(parts, groupID)
687		} else if resultRev := correlation["result_rev"]; resultRev != "" {
688			parts = append(parts, resultRev)
689		}
690		if reviewerIndex := correlation["reviewer_index"]; reviewerIndex != "" {
691			parts = append(parts, reviewerIndex)
692		}
693		if len(parts) == 0 {
694			return ""
695		}
696		return strings.Join(parts, ":")
697	}

699	// reviewPairingDegradedBody renders the kind:review-pairing-degraded event
700	// body. The body carries both implementer and reviewer routing details so
701	// operators can see exactly which routes converged.
702	func reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl ImplementerRouting, reviewerHarness, reviewerProvider, reviewerModel string, reviewerPower int) string {
703		var b strings.Builder
704		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_harness=%s\n", impl.Harness)
705		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_provider=%s\n", impl.Provider)
706		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_model=%s\n", impl.Model)
707		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "impl_actual_power=%d\n", impl.ActualPower)
708		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_harness=%s\n", reviewerHarness)
709		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_provider=%s\n", reviewerProvider)
710		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_model=%s\n", reviewerModel)
711		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "reviewer_actual_power=%d\n", reviewerPower)
712		fmt.Fprintf(&b, "min_power_requested=%d\n", impl.ActualPower+1)
713		return b.String()
714	}

716	// ReviewBead implements BeadReviewer.
717	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) ReviewBead(ctx context.Context, beadID, resultRev string, impl ImplementerRouting) (*ReviewResult, error) {
718		b, err := r.BeadStore.Get(beadID)
719		if err != nil {
720			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: get bead %s: %w", beadID, err)
721		}
722	
723		// Fetch the git diff for the commit being reviewed.
724		diff, err := r.gitShow(resultRev)
725		if err != nil {
726			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: git show %s: %w", resultRev, err)
727		}
728	
729		// Resolve governing document references.
730		refs := ResolveGoverningRefs(r.ProjectRoot, b)
731	
732		// Determine iteration number from bead events.
733		iter := 1
734	
735		// Build the review prompt under evidence caps.
736		caps := r.Caps
737		if caps.MaxPromptBytes == 0 {
738			caps = evidence.DefaultCaps()
739		}
740		built := BuildReviewPromptBounded(b, iter, resultRev, diff, r.ProjectRoot, refs, BuildReviewPromptOptions{Caps: caps})
741		prompt := built.Prompt
742		attemptID := GenerateAttemptID()
743		artifacts, err := createArtifactBundle(r.ProjectRoot, r.ProjectRoot, attemptID)
744		if err != nil {
745			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: create artifact bundle: %w", err)
746		}
747		if err := os.WriteFile(artifacts.PromptAbs, []byte(prompt), 0o644); err != nil {
748			return nil, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: write prompt artifact: %w", err)
749		}
750	
751		// Pre-dispatch short-circuit (FEAT-022 §7): if the assembled prompt
752		// exceeds MaxPromptBytes after all per-section trimming, skip the
753		// provider dispatch and return a typed review-error: context_overflow.
754		// The bead is NOT closed — the loop's review-error event-append path
755		// records the failure and leaves the bead open for retry.
756		if built.Overflow {
757			baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
758			overflowTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
759				Sections:    built.Sections,
760				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
761				OutputBytes: 0,
762				Harness:     r.Harness,
763				Model:       r.Model,
764			}
765			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
766				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
767				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
768				Rationale:       evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
769				ReviewerHarness: r.Harness,
770				ReviewerModel:   r.Model,
771				BaseRev:         baseRev,
772				ResultRev:       resultRev,
773				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
774				CostUSD:         0,
775				InputBytes:      overflowTelemetry.InputBytes,
776				OutputBytes:     overflowTelemetry.OutputBytes,
777			}
778			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
779				Harness:          r.Harness,
780				Model:            r.Model,
781				BaseRev:          baseRev,
782				ResultRev:        resultRev,
783				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
784				BeadID:           beadID,
785				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
786				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
787			}, reviewArtifactResult{
788				Verdict:          string(VerdictBlock),
789				Error:            evidence.OutcomeReviewContextOverflow,
790				EvidenceAssembly: overflowTelemetry,
791			})
792			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: PROMPT_BUDGET_EXCEEDED/context_overflow (assembled prompt %d bytes exceeds cap %d; see %s)",
793				len(prompt), caps.MaxPromptBytes, artifacts.DirRel)
794		}
795	
796		// Resolve reviewer harness and model. An explicit reviewer harness wins;
797		// otherwise the reviewer follows the implementation harness. If both are
798		// empty, leave routing unconstrained and let the agent service choose within
799		// the MinPower bound.
800		reviewHarness := r.Harness
801		if reviewHarness == "" {
802			reviewHarness = impl.Harness
803		}
804		reviewModel := r.Model
805		if reviewModel == "" && reviewHarness != "" {
806			reviewModel = ResolveModelTier(reviewHarness, SelectReviewerTier(escalation.TierSmart))
807		}
808	
809		start := time.Now()
810		runRuntime := BuildReviewExecuteRequest(impl, reviewHarness, reviewModel)
811		runRuntime.Prompt = prompt
812		runRuntime.WorkDir = r.ProjectRoot
813		result, runErr := r.dispatchReviewRun(ctx, runRuntime)
814	
815		durationMS := int(time.Since(start).Milliseconds())
816		if runErr != nil {
817			// Transport-class failure (FEAT-022 §12): network or provider-side
818			// error. Surface as a typed review-error so the loop classifies and
819			// counts it correctly, rather than masquerading as a BLOCK verdict.
820			transportTelemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
821				Sections:    built.Sections,
822				InputBytes:  len(prompt),
823				OutputBytes: 0,
824				ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
825				Harness:     reviewHarness,
826				Model:       reviewModel,
827			}
828			reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
829				Verdict:         VerdictBlock,
830				Rationale:       runErr.Error(),
831				Error:           evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport,
832				ReviewerHarness: reviewHarness,
833				ReviewerModel:   reviewModel,
834				BaseRev:         resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev),
835				ResultRev:       resultRev,
836				ExecutionDir:    artifacts.DirRel,
837				DurationMS:      durationMS,
838				CostUSD:         resultCost(result),
839				InputBytes:      transportTelemetry.InputBytes,
840				OutputBytes:     transportTelemetry.OutputBytes,
841			}
842			_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
843				Harness:          reviewHarness,
844				Model:            reviewModel,
845				BaseRev:          reviewRes.BaseRev,
846				ResultRev:        resultRev,
847				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
848				BeadID:           beadID,
849				ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
850				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
851			}, reviewArtifactResult{
852				Verdict:          string(reviewRes.Verdict),
853				Rationale:        reviewRes.Rationale,
854				Error:            reviewRes.Error,
855				EvidenceAssembly: transportTelemetry,
856			})
857			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w", evidence.OutcomeReviewTransport, runErr)
858		}
859	
860		actualHarness := reviewHarness
861		actualModel := reviewModel
862		actualProvider := ""
863		actualPower := 0
864		if result != nil {
865			if result.Harness != "" {
866				actualHarness = result.Harness
867			}
868			if result.Model != "" {
869				actualModel = result.Model
870			}
871			actualProvider = result.Provider
872			actualPower = result.ActualPower
873			durationMS = result.DurationMS
874		}
875	
876		output := ""
877		sessionID := ""
878		if result != nil {
879			output = result.Output
880			sessionID = result.AgentSessionID
881		}
882		// Strict JSON parse: replaces the legacy markdown extractor that silently
883		// pulled "BLOCK" from the prompt's options-header line whenever the model
884		// echoed it back (the upstream-report regression). On parse error we emit
885		// a typed review-error class — the execute-loop leaves the bead open for
886		// retry rather than mis-recording a BLOCK verdict.
887		parsed, parseErr := ParseReviewVerdict([]byte(output))
888		var strictVerdict Verdict
889		var rationale string
890		var findings []Finding
891		if parseErr == nil {
892			strictVerdict = parsed.Verdict
893			rationale = strings.TrimSpace(parsed.Summary)
894			findings = parsed.Findings
895			if rationale == "" && len(findings) > 0 {
896				parts := make([]string, 0, len(findings))
897				for _, f := range findings {
898					line := strings.TrimSpace(f.Summary)
899					if line == "" {
900						continue
901					}
902					if f.Location != "" {
903						line = f.Location + ": " + line
904					}
905					parts = append(parts, line)
906				}
907				rationale = strings.Join(parts, "\n")
908			}
909		}
910		baseRev := resolveReviewBaseRev(r.ProjectRoot, resultRev)
911		telemetry := &EvidenceAssemblyTelemetry{
912			Sections:    built.Sections,
913			InputBytes:  len(prompt),
914			OutputBytes: len(output),
915			ElapsedMS:   durationMS,
916			Harness:     actualHarness,
917			Model:       actualModel,
918		}
919		reviewRes := &ReviewResult{
920			Verdict:          strictVerdict,
921			Rationale:        rationale,
922			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
923			RawOutput:        output,
924			ReviewerHarness:  actualHarness,
925			ReviewerModel:    actualModel,
926			ReviewerProvider: actualProvider,
927			SessionID:        sessionID,
928			BaseRev:          baseRev,
929			ResultRev:        resultRev,
930			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
931			DurationMS:       durationMS,
932			CostUSD:          resultCost(result),
933			InputBytes:       telemetry.InputBytes,
934			OutputBytes:      telemetry.OutputBytes,
935		}
936	
937		// R4 pairing-degradation telemetry: when MinPower=actualPower+1 fails
938		// to push the reviewer onto a different provider than the implementer,
939		// surface a kind:review-pairing-degraded event so operators can see the
940		// regression. Best-effort only — emission failures never affect the
941		// review outcome itself.
942		if r.BeadEvents != nil && actualProvider != "" && impl.Provider != "" && actualProvider == impl.Provider {
943			_ = r.BeadEvents.AppendEvent(beadID, bead.BeadEvent{
944				Kind:      ReviewPairingDegradedEventKind,
945				Summary:   fmt.Sprintf("reviewer pinned to same provider as implementer (%s)", impl.Provider),
946				Body:      reviewPairingDegradedBody(impl, actualHarness, actualProvider, actualModel, actualPower),
947				Source:    "ddx agent execute-loop",
948				CreatedAt: time.Now().UTC(),
949			})
950		}
951		_ = writeReviewArtifacts(artifacts, reviewArtifactManifest{
952			Harness:          actualHarness,
953			Model:            actualModel,
954			SessionID:        sessionID,
955			BaseRev:          baseRev,
956			ResultRev:        resultRev,
957			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
958			BeadID:           beadID,
959			ExecutionDir:     artifacts.DirRel,
960			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
961		}, reviewArtifactResult{
962			Verdict:          string(strictVerdict),
963			Rationale:        rationale,
964			Findings:         findings,
965			ProseFindings:    parsed.ProseFindings,
966			Error:            reviewRes.Error,
967			EvidenceAssembly: telemetry,
968		})
969		if parseErr != nil {
970			// FEAT-022 §12: distinguish provider_empty (zero bytes) from
971			// unparseable (text without a recognizable JSON contract). Operators
972			// triage these differently — provider_empty often signals a context-
973			// window or backend availability issue, while unparseable signals a
974			// reviewer-prompt or output-format drift.
975			class := evidence.OutcomeReviewUnparseable
976			if strings.TrimSpace(output) == "" {
977				class = evidence.OutcomeReviewProviderEmpty
978			}
979			reviewRes.Error = class
980			return reviewRes, fmt.Errorf("reviewer: %s: %w (raw output %d bytes; see %s)", class, parseErr, len(output), artifacts.DirRel)
981		}
982		return reviewRes, nil
983	}

985	// dispatchReviewRun is a thin SD-024 wrapper around dispatchViaResolvedConfig
986	// for the post-merge reviewer. The reviewer carries no persistent
987	// ResolvedConfig of its own — the durable knobs that affect a review
988	// invocation (timeout, provider, evidence caps) are read from the project's
989	// .ddx/config.yaml via LoadAndResolve, while reviewer-tier harness/model
990	// are pinned via the runtime override fields. Resolution order matches the
991	// execute-bead worker (runner > pre-built service > fresh service).
992	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) dispatchReviewRun(ctx context.Context, runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error) {

997	// gitShow runs `git show <rev>` with pathspec exclusions for execution-
998	// evidence noise so the review prompt's <diff> section stays bounded even
999	// when an old commit tracked a multi-thousand-line session log. See
1000	// EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs and ddx-39e27896 for the regression.
1001	func (r *DefaultBeadReviewer) gitShow(rev string) (string, error) {
1002		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 60*time.Second)
1003		defer cancel()
1004		args := append([]string{"show", rev, "--", "."}, EvidenceReviewExcludePathspecs()...)
1005		out, err := internalgit.Command(ctx, r.ProjectRoot, args...).Output()
1006		if err != nil {
1007			return "", err
1008		}
1009		return string(out), nil
1010	}

1013		if resultRev == "" {

1017		if err != nil {
1018			return ""
1019		}
1020		return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
1021	}
